step_by_step_thinking:
I concur with the decision to choose option C ("Touch"). While it is true that nociceptive fibers can lose function relatively quickly due to their less myelinated state, the broader involvement of mechanoreceptors responsible for detecting different forms of touch results in a more widespread impairment almost simultaneously. Given how closely related many tactile sensations are within our nervous system, losing them together makes practical sense from both functional and diagnostic perspectives. Therefore, sticking with Option C aligns well with understanding the overall pattern of sensory deprivation post-anesthesia application.
